child.name: resourceType
child.description: This is a ConceptMap resource

child.name: id
child.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

child.name: meta
child.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not alwa...
child.longDesc: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: versionId
child.description: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is create...
child.longDesc: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is created, updated, or deleted.

child.name: lastUpdated
child.description: When the resource last changed - e.g. when the version changed.

child.name: source
child.description: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [[[Provenance]]] information that can...
child.longDesc: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [[[Provenance]]] information that can be used to track or differentiate the source of information in the resource. The source may identify another FHIR server, document, message, database, etc.

child.name: profile
child.description: A list of profiles (references to [[[StructureDefinition]]] resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a ref...
child.longDesc: A list of profiles (references to [[[StructureDefinition]]] resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a reference to [[[StructureDefinition.url]]].

child.name: security
child.description: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructur...
child.longDesc: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructure.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: tag
child.description: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applic...
child.longDesc: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applications are not required to consider the tags when interpreting the meaning of a resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: implicitRules
child.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing...
child.longDesc: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

child.name: language
child.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

child.name: text
child.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a...
child.longDesc: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: status
child.description: The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too), or whether a h...
child.longDesc: The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too), or whether a human authored it and it may contain additional data.

child.name: div
child.description: The actual narrative content, a stripped down version of XHTML.

child.name: url
child.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this concept map when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance...
child.longDesc: An absolute URI that is used to identify this concept map when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which an authoritative instance of this concept map is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the concept map is stored on different servers.

child.name: identifier
child.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this concept map when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specif...
child.longDesc: A formal identifier that is used to identify this concept map when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: version
child.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the concept map when it is referenced in a specification, model, design ...
child.longDesc: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the concept map when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the concept map author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

child.name: versionAlgorithmString
child.description: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which ConceptMap is more current.

child.name: versionAlgorithmCoding
child.description: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which ConceptMap is more current.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: name
child.description: A natural language name identifying the concept map. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine proce...
child.longDesc: A natural language name identifying the concept map. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

child.name: title
child.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the concept map.

child.name: status
child.description: The status of this concept map. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

child.name: experimental
child.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this concept map is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is no...
child.longDesc: A Boolean value to indicate that this concept map is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

child.name: date
child.description: The date (and optionally time) when the concept map was last significantly changed. The date must change when the business vers...
child.longDesc: The date (and optionally time) when the concept map was last significantly changed. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the concept map changes.

child.name: publisher
child.description: The name of the organization or individual responsible for the release and ongoing maintenance of the concept map.

child.name: contact
child.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: name
child.description: The name of an individual to contact.

child.name: telecom
child.description: The contact details for the individual (if a name was provided) or the organization.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: Telecommunications form for contact point - what communications system is required to make use of the contact.

child.name: value
child.description: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or emai...
child.longDesc: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or email address).

child.name: use
child.description: Identifies the purpose for the contact point.

child.name: rank
child.description: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than thos...
child.longDesc: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than those with higher rank values.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period when the contact point was/is in use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: description
child.description: A free text natural language description of the concept map from a consumer's perspective.

child.name: useContext
child.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general cate...
child.longDesc: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate concept map instances.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: A code that identifies the type of context being specified by this usage context.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: valueCodeableConcept
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: valueQuantity
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: valueRange
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: low
child.description: The low limit. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: high
child.description: The high limit. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: valueReference
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: jurisdiction
child.description: A legal or geographic region in which the concept map is intended to be used.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: purpose
child.description: Explanation of why this concept map is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

child.name: copyright
child.description: A copyright statement relating to the concept map and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on ...
child.longDesc: A copyright statement relating to the concept map and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the concept map.

child.name: copyrightLabel
child.description: A short string (<50 characters), suitable for inclusion in a page footer that identifies the copyright holder, effective period,...
child.longDesc: A short string (<50 characters), suitable for inclusion in a page footer that identifies the copyright holder, effective period, and optionally whether rights are resctricted. (e.g. 'All rights reserved', 'Some rights reserved').

child.name: approvalDate
child.description: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially appro...
child.longDesc: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

child.name: lastReviewDate
child.description: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the ori...
child.longDesc: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

child.name: effectivePeriod
child.description: The period during which the ConceptMap content was or is planned to be in active use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: topic
child.description: Descriptions related to the content of the ConceptMap. Topics provide a high-level categorization as well as keywords for the Co...
child.longDesc: Descriptions related to the content of the ConceptMap. Topics provide a high-level categorization as well as keywords for the ConceptMap that can be useful for filtering and searching.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: author
child.description: An individiual or organization primarily involved in the creation and maintenance of the ConceptMap.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: name
child.description: The name of an individual to contact.

child.name: telecom
child.description: The contact details for the individual (if a name was provided) or the organization.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: Telecommunications form for contact point - what communications system is required to make use of the contact.

child.name: value
child.description: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or emai...
child.longDesc: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or email address).

child.name: use
child.description: Identifies the purpose for the contact point.

child.name: rank
child.description: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than thos...
child.longDesc: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than those with higher rank values.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period when the contact point was/is in use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: editor
child.description: An individual or organization primarily responsible for internal coherence of the ConceptMap.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: name
child.description: The name of an individual to contact.

child.name: telecom
child.description: The contact details for the individual (if a name was provided) or the organization.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: Telecommunications form for contact point - what communications system is required to make use of the contact.

child.name: value
child.description: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or emai...
child.longDesc: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or email address).

child.name: use
child.description: Identifies the purpose for the contact point.

child.name: rank
child.description: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than thos...
child.longDesc: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than those with higher rank values.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period when the contact point was/is in use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: reviewer
child.description: An individual or organization asserted by the publisher to be primarily responsible for review of some aspect of the ConceptMap.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: name
child.description: The name of an individual to contact.

child.name: telecom
child.description: The contact details for the individual (if a name was provided) or the organization.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: Telecommunications form for contact point - what communications system is required to make use of the contact.

child.name: value
child.description: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or emai...
child.longDesc: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or email address).

child.name: use
child.description: Identifies the purpose for the contact point.

child.name: rank
child.description: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than thos...
child.longDesc: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than those with higher rank values.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period when the contact point was/is in use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: endorser
child.description: An individual or organization asserted by the publisher to be responsible for officially endorsing the ConceptMap for use in som...
child.longDesc: An individual or organization asserted by the publisher to be responsible for officially endorsing the ConceptMap for use in some setting.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: name
child.description: The name of an individual to contact.

child.name: telecom
child.description: The contact details for the individual (if a name was provided) or the organization.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: Telecommunications form for contact point - what communications system is required to make use of the contact.

child.name: value
child.description: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or emai...
child.longDesc: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or email address).

child.name: use
child.description: Identifies the purpose for the contact point.

child.name: rank
child.description: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than thos...
child.longDesc: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than those with higher rank values.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period when the contact point was/is in use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: relatedArtifact
child.description: Related artifacts such as additional documentation, justification, dependencies, bibliographic references, and predecessor and s...

savedDerivedCopy.name: relatedArtifact
savedDerivedCopy.description: Related artifacts such as additional documentation, justification, dependencies, bibliographic references, and predecessor and s...

child.name: property
child.description: A property defines a slot through which additional information can be provided about a map from source -> target.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: A code that is used to identify the property. The code is used internally (in ConceptMap.group.element.target.property.code) and...
child.longDesc: A code that is used to identify the property. The code is used internally (in ConceptMap.group.element.target.property.code) and also in the $translate operation.

child.name: uri
child.description: Reference to the formal meaning of the property.

child.name: description
child.description: A description of the property - why it is defined, and how its value might be used.

child.name: type
child.description: The type of the property value.

child.name: system
child.description: The CodeSystem that defines the codes from which values of type ```code``` in property values.

child.name: additionalAttribute
child.description: An additionalAttribute defines an additional data element found in the source or target data model where the data will come from...
child.longDesc: An additionalAttribute defines an additional data element found in the source or target data model where the data will come from or be mapped to. Some mappings are based on data in addition to the source data element, where codes in multiple fields are combined to a single field (or vice versa).

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: A code that is used to identify this additional data attribute. The code is used internally in ConceptMap.group.element.target.d...
child.longDesc: A code that is used to identify this additional data attribute. The code is used internally in ConceptMap.group.element.target.dependsOn.attribute and ConceptMap.group.element.target.product.attribute.

child.name: uri
child.description: Reference to the formal definition of the source/target data element. For elements defined by the FHIR specification, or using a...
child.longDesc: Reference to the formal definition of the source/target data element. For elements defined by the FHIR specification, or using a FHIR logical model, the correct format is {canonical-url}#{element-id}.

child.name: description
child.description: A description of the additional attribute and/or the data element it refers to - why it is defined, and how the value might be u...
child.longDesc: A description of the additional attribute and/or the data element it refers to - why it is defined, and how the value might be used in mappings, and a discussion of issues associated with the use of the data element.

child.name: type
child.description: The type of the source data contained in this concept map for this data element.

child.name: sourceScopeUri
child.description: Identifier for the source value set that contains the concepts that are being mapped and provides context for the mappings. Lim...
child.longDesc: Identifier for the source value set that contains the concepts that are being mapped and provides context for the mappings. Limits the scope of the map to source codes (ConceptMap.group.element code or valueSet) that are members of this value set.

child.name: sourceScopeCanonical
child.description: Identifier for the source value set that contains the concepts that are being mapped and provides context for the mappings. Lim...
child.longDesc: Identifier for the source value set that contains the concepts that are being mapped and provides context for the mappings. Limits the scope of the map to source codes (ConceptMap.group.element code or valueSet) that are members of this value set.

child.name: targetScopeUri
child.description: Identifier for the target value set that provides important context about how the mapping choices are made. Limits the scope of...
child.longDesc: Identifier for the target value set that provides important context about how the mapping choices are made. Limits the scope of the map to target codes (ConceptMap.group.element.target code or valueSet) that are members of this value set.

child.name: targetScopeCanonical
child.description: Identifier for the target value set that provides important context about how the mapping choices are made. Limits the scope of...
child.longDesc: Identifier for the target value set that provides important context about how the mapping choices are made. Limits the scope of the map to target codes (ConceptMap.group.element.target code or valueSet) that are members of this value set.

child.name: group
child.description: A group of mappings that all have the same source and target system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: source
child.description: An absolute URI that identifies the source system where the concepts to be mapped are defined.

child.name: target
child.description: An absolute URI that identifies the target system that the concepts will be mapped to.

child.name: element
child.description: Mappings for an individual concept in the source to one or more concepts in the target.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: Identity (code or path) or the element/item being mapped.

child.name: display
child.description: The display for the code. The display is only provided to help editors when editing the concept map.

child.name: valueSet
child.description: The set of concepts from the ConceptMap.group.source code system which are all being mapped to the target as part of this mappin...
child.longDesc: The set of concepts from the ConceptMap.group.source code system which are all being mapped to the target as part of this mapping rule.

child.name: noMap
child.description: If noMap = true this indicates that no mapping to a target concept exists for this source concept.

child.name: target
child.description: A concept from the target value set that this concept maps to.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: Identity (code or path) or the element/item that the map refers to.

child.name: display
child.description: The display for the code. The display is only provided to help editors when editing the concept map.

child.name: valueSet
child.description: The set of concepts from the ConceptMap.group.target code system which are all being mapped to as part of this mapping rule. The...
child.longDesc: The set of concepts from the ConceptMap.group.target code system which are all being mapped to as part of this mapping rule. The effect of using this data element is the same as having multiple ConceptMap.group.element.target elements with one for each concept in the ConceptMap.group.element.target.valueSet value set.

child.name: relationship
child.description: The relationship between the source and target concepts. The relationship is read from source to target (e.g. source-is-narrower...
child.longDesc: The relationship between the source and target concepts. The relationship is read from source to target (e.g. source-is-narrower-than-target).

child.name: comment
child.description: A description of status/issues in mapping that conveys additional information not represented in the structured data.

child.name: property
child.description: A property value for this source -> target mapping.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: A reference to a mapping property defined in ConceptMap.property.

child.name: valueCoding
child.description: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.pr...
child.longDesc: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.property element.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: valueString
child.description: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.pr...
child.longDesc: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.property element.

child.name: valueInteger
child.description: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.pr...
child.longDesc: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.property element.

child.name: valueBoolean
child.description: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.pr...
child.longDesc: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.property element.

child.name: valueDateTime
child.description: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.pr...
child.longDesc: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.property element.

child.name: valueDecimal
child.description: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.pr...
child.longDesc: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.property element.

child.name: valueCode
child.description: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.pr...
child.longDesc: The value of this property. If the type chosen for this element is 'code', then the property SHALL be defined in a ConceptMap.property element.

child.name: dependsOn
child.description: A set of additional dependencies for this mapping to hold. This mapping is only applicable if the specified data attribute can b...
child.longDesc: A set of additional dependencies for this mapping to hold. This mapping is only applicable if the specified data attribute can be resolved, and it has the specified value.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: attribute
child.description: A reference to the additional attribute that holds a value the map depends on.

child.name: valueCode
child.description: Data element value that the map depends on / produces.

child.name: valueCoding
child.description: Data element value that the map depends on / produces.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: valueString
child.description: Data element value that the map depends on / produces.

child.name: valueBoolean
child.description: Data element value that the map depends on / produces.

child.name: valueQuantity
child.description: Data element value that the map depends on / produces.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: valueSet
child.description: This mapping applies if the data element value is a code from this value set.

child.name: product
child.description: Product is the output of a ConceptMap that provides additional values that go in other attributes / data elemnts of the target d...
child.longDesc: Product is the output of a ConceptMap that provides additional values that go in other attributes / data elemnts of the target data.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: attribute
child.description: A reference to the additional attribute that holds a value the map depends on.

child.name: valueCode
child.description: Data element value that the map depends on / produces.

child.name: valueCoding
child.description: Data element value that the map depends on / produces.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: valueString
child.description: Data element value that the map depends on / produces.

child.name: valueBoolean
child.description: Data element value that the map depends on / produces.

child.name: valueQuantity
child.description: Data element value that the map depends on / produces.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: valueSet
child.description: This mapping applies if the data element value is a code from this value set.

child.name: unmapped
child.description: What to do when there is no mapping to a target concept from the source concept and ConceptMap.group.element.noMap is not true. ...
child.longDesc: What to do when there is no mapping to a target concept from the source concept and ConceptMap.group.element.noMap is not true. This provides the "default" to be applied when there is no target concept mapping specified or the expansion of ConceptMap.group.element.target.valueSet is empty.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: mode
child.description: Defines which action to take if there is no match for the source concept in the target system designated for the group. One of 3...
child.longDesc: Defines which action to take if there is no match for the source concept in the target system designated for the group. One of 3 actions are possible: use the unmapped source code (this is useful when doing a mapping between versions, and only a few codes have changed), use a fixed code (a default code), or alternatively, a reference to a different concept map can be provided (by canonical URL).

child.name: code
child.description: The fixed code to use when the mode = 'fixed' - all unmapped codes are mapped to a single fixed code.

child.name: display
child.description: The display for the code. The display is only provided to help editors when editing the concept map.

child.name: valueSet
child.description: The set of fixed codes to use when the mode = 'fixed' - all unmapped codes are mapped to each of the fixed codes.

child.name: relationship
child.description: The default relationship value to apply between the source and target concepts when the source code is unmapped and the mode is ...
child.longDesc: The default relationship value to apply between the source and target concepts when the source code is unmapped and the mode is 'fixed' or 'use-source-code'.

child.name: otherMap
child.description: The canonical reference to an additional ConceptMap resource instance to use for mapping if this ConceptMap resource contains no...
child.longDesc: The canonical reference to an additional ConceptMap resource instance to use for mapping if this ConceptMap resource contains no matching mapping for the source concept.
